name: Cold-Summer Subarctic
precipitationPattern: Year-round low precipitation; snow dominant in winter
vegetation: Boreal forest (taiga), spruce, pine, and fir; mosses and lichens
description: Subarctic climate (Dfc) is the world's most extensive climate type by area, stretching across the vast boreal forests of Canada, Scandinavia, and Siberia. The taiga biome under this climate represents the largest terrestrial biome on Earth. Winters are long and cold, summers brief but with very long daylight hours.
typicalWeather: Long, cold winters (-15 to -35°C) lasting 5-7 months. Brief, cool summers (12-18°C) with 18-24 hours of daylight. Moderate precipitation (300-600mm) with much falling as snow. Lakes and rivers freeze for months. The aurora borealis is frequently visible.
